title: carex flava subsp. marssonii
intro: Carex flava subsp. marssonii, also known as variegated sedge, is a subspecies of the Carex flava plant, a type of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Carex flava subsp. marssonii include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against infections. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
